vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but incomplete. P3 (Pothos with rabbit) and P1 (Yellow pot) are present. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are absent or in a state of total systemic collapse, appearing as empty soil.

plant_audit.P1: Appears as a single, solitary leaf in a black pot. No signs of active growth.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen is severely diminished; only one leaf remains visible near the rabbit anchor.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Zone: The yellow pot is devoid of visible plant matter, indicating a failure of the specimens previously assigned to this biome.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp appearance with significant debris (crushed eggshells) present in the black pot.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot suggests an intervention (calcium supplementation or pest deterrent attempt).

temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-3: Gradual loss of structural integrity in the P3 Pothos leaf; slight yellowing/chlorosis noted.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_current: The P3 leaf has become more translucent and limp. The yellow pot remains consistently empty throughout the sequence.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical decline. The Pothos (P3) is suffering from severe physiological stress, likely due to light deficiency or root-zone issues. The empty pots (P2/P4) indicate a total loss of the intended specimens.
anomalies: Crushed eggshells used as soil top-dressing.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a struggling indoor garden. The Pothos in the black pot is the only remaining biological occupant, and it is in a state of advanced senescence. The yellow pot, intended for P1, is currently empty, and the black pot (P2/P4) shows no signs of life. The environment is characterized by high humidity or poor drainage (dark soil) and a lack of sufficient light to support the remaining foliage.
